Reports claim Kevin Mirallas is not needed at Everton so should Newcastle be ready to pounce?



When Kevin Mirallas signed a new three-year contract on May 12 it seemed, perhaps understandably, that Everton viewed the winger as part of their long-term plans.


Yet not according to talkSPORT, who claim that Mirallas is in fact not part of Ronald Koeman's plans and could well leave Goodison Park this summer.

Reports from Turkish publication, Fanatik, have already suggested that the Super Lig could be the 29-year-old's next destination, with Fenerbahce keeping close tabs on developments.

Yet if this is true and Mirallas will be leaving the Toffees soon, would the Belgian not be the ideal signing for newly promoted Newcastle United?


After winning the Championship in 2016/17 and the promise of big spending ahead of their return to the top flight, the Magpies have still made just one permanent signing this summer in Christian Atsu - who spent the last 12 months on loan at St James' Park from Chelsea in any case.

Joel Robles has rejected a contract extension at Everton in the hopes of returning to his native Spain, according to Marca.
The 26-year-old 'keeper hinted at the end of the season that he wasn't sure he would be staying at Goodison Park and the arrival of Jordan Pickford as the presumptive new "No 1" at Everton has likely made up his mind for him,

Málaga, Deportivo La Coruña, Las Palmas and Getafe are all mentioned as possible destinations for Robles who won and then seemed to lose the first choice 'keeper's slot under Ronald Koeman last season.

Robles, a £4.5m capture from Atletico Madrid four years ago, has a year remaining on his current contract.

Original Source: Marca
